hidden_gems.name: Domaine Sylvain Pataille
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Trained as an oenologist, Sylvain Pataille built his Marsannay domaine from 1999 and is the leading champion of the northernmost Cote de Nuits village. Ecocert-certified since 2008, he bottles a celebrated range of single-lieu-dit Aligote that has done more than anyone to rehabilitate the grape.
hidden_gems.tip: Pataille's lieu-dit Aligotes (Le Clou, Champ Foret) are the wines insiders chase; the Marsannay reds and rose round out a village few visitors think to seek.
hidden_gems.description: Marsannay reference producer farming organically since 2008. Sylvain Pataille's single-parcel Aligote bottlings have made the overlooked grape a collector's interest.

hidden_gems.name: Domaine Hubert Lamy
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Olivier Lamy runs this family estate dating to 1640 with around 16.5 hectares, mostly in Saint-Aubin alongside parcels in Chassagne-Montrachet and a sliver of Grand Cru Criots-Batard-Montrachet. His high-density planting and precise whites from Saint-Aubin's Premiers Crus rival far pricier Puligny neighbours.
hidden_gems.tip: Saint-Aubin's Premier Cru Murgers des Dents de Chien adjoins Puligny's best slopes; Lamy's bottlings deliver Cote de Beaune class at a fraction of Montrachet money.
hidden_gems.description: A 1640-founded Saint-Aubin estate run by Olivier Lamy, whose high-density whites from Premier Cru vineyards next to Puligny-Montrachet are among Burgundy's great value finds.

hidden_gems.name: Domaine Joblot
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Juliette Joblot succeeded her father Jean-Marc and uncle Vincent in 2012 at this 14-hectare Givry estate widely credited with putting the Cote Chalonnaise village on the map. The Premier Cru reds (Servoisine, Cellier aux Moines, Bois Chevaux) are dense, polished Pinot Noir at Cote Chalonnaise prices.
hidden_gems.tip: Givry was once the favourite wine of King Henri IV; Joblot's Premier Cru reds are the bottles that prove the village still deserves its old reputation.
hidden_gems.description: The Givry estate, now led by Juliette Joblot, credited with reviving the Cote Chalonnaise village. Polished Premier Cru Pinot Noir at gentle prices.

hidden_gems.name: Domaine Francois Lumpp
hidden_gems.address: Le Pied du Clou, 36 Avenue de Mortieres, 71640 Givry, France
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Francois Lumpp founded his own Givry estate in 1991 and, with his family, built a cult following for precise, mineral whites and elegant reds. Alongside Joblot, Lumpp is the name local sommeliers reach for when they want to show Givry at its best.
hidden_gems.tip: Lumpp's Premier Cru Clos du Cras Long and Petit Marole are the bottlings to seek; production is small, so buy at the cellar when you can.
hidden_gems.description: A Givry estate founded in 1991 by Francois Lumpp, making precise mineral whites and elegant reds that rank among the Cote Chalonnaise's finest.

hidden_gems.name: Domaine Vincent Dureuil-Janthial
hidden_gems.address: 10 Rue de la Buisserolle, 71150 Rully, France
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Vincent and Celine Dureuil took over in 1994 and turned this 20-hectare Rully domaine into the reference of the appellation, with parcels reaching up into Puligny-Montrachet and Nuits-Saint-Georges. The old-vine Rully whites have a depth that embarrasses many Cote d'Or villages.
hidden_gems.tip: Rully is the Cote Chalonnaise's best-kept white-wine secret; Dureuil-Janthial's Vieilles Vignes is the bottle that explains why insiders rate the village.
hidden_gems.description: Rully's reference domaine, run by Vincent and Celine Dureuil since 1994. Old-vine Chardonnay with Cote d'Or depth at Cote Chalonnaise prices.

hidden_gems.name: Domaine du Comte Armand
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Sole owner since 1828 of the monopole Clos des Epeneaux in Pommard, the domaine has farmed biodynamically since the early 1990s and is Ecocert-certified organic. The single-walled Premier Cru, vinified across vine-age parcels, is one of the Cote de Beaune's most structured, ageworthy reds.
hidden_gems.tip: The Clos des Epeneaux is a true monopole; ask about the old-vine versus young-vine components that go into the final blend each year.
hidden_gems.description: Pommard estate and sole owner of the Clos des Epeneaux monopole since 1828, farming biodynamically. A structured, long-lived Premier Cru that flies below the Cote de Nuits radar.

hidden_gems.name: Domaine A. et P. de Villaine
hidden_gems.address: 2 Rue de la Fontaine, 71150 Bouzeron, France
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Run by Aubert de Villaine, co-director of Domaine de la Romanee-Conti, this organic Bouzeron estate elevated Aligote into a serious wine. The Bouzeron bottling from the only AOC reserved for the grape shows what unhurried farming and old vines can do for an unfashionable variety.
hidden_gems.tip: Few visitors realise the man behind Romanee-Conti pours one of Burgundy's best-value whites here; the Bouzeron and the Cote Chalonnaise reds reward a detour.
hidden_gems.description: Aubert de Villaine's organic Bouzeron estate, where the co-director of Romanee-Conti makes the benchmark Aligote from the grape's own appellation.

hidden_gems.name: Domaine Trapet Pere et Fils
hidden_gems.what_locals_love: Jean-Louis Trapet farms this historic Gevrey-Chambertin domaine biodynamically and holds Demeter certification, with holdings in the Grands Crus Chambertin and Latricieres-Chambertin. The wines are perfumed and transparent, a quieter, more spiritual face of Gevrey than the village's bigger names.
hidden_gems.tip: Trapet's village Gevrey-Chambertin and Marsannay bottlings are the affordable way into a domaine whose Grand Cru Chambertin sits among the appellation's references.
hidden_gems.description: Demeter-certified Gevrey-Chambertin estate making perfumed, transparent Pinot Noir from Grand Cru Chambertin down to value Marsannay.
